memory.summary: I'm way too stressed about this project, but I really need to hang out with my friends right now instead of working.
My friends are my only escape from this project stress. I can't believe I'm already texting them instead of working.
I let the moment pass again, just like always. At least I didn't have to deal with anyone or anything stressful.
Sending that meme to cohort_3 felt so much better than stressing about the project. I'm just glad we're all laughing together right now.
I'm already texting my friends about the new assignment instead of actually reading it. The stress is real, but at least we're in this together.
I'm still just texting my friends instead of starting the project, but at least we're laughing together. The afternoon is slipping away, but dealing with the work feels too stressful right now.
Sending that meme to cohort_3 felt so much better than stressing about the project. I'm just glad we're all laughing together right now.
Sending that cat video to the group felt like a perfect escape from the stress. Just laughing with them makes the whole situation feel manageable for now.
I just texted the group about that weird glitch, hoping they see it too. If we all laugh about it together, maybe I won't have to think about my project right now.
Their excitement is contagious! Seeing them so hyped makes me feel like I'm part of the fun again, which is way better than worrying about my project.
Wait, did this actually happen to Sara? If it's the same glitch, maybe we should all just panic together instead of stressing alone.
They totally get it! Sharing this panic feels so much better than stressing alone, even if I'm ignoring my project again.

desires: Keep the group chat laughing and engaged so I don't have to face my project alone
Avoid any awkwardness or conflict by staying in sync with my friends' excitement
Stay in this moment of shared fun rather than thinking about the consequences of ignoring my deadlines

goals.description: Immediately reply to the next message in cohort_3 with high energy and validation to maintain the group's momentum.
goals.rationale: My affiliation value (0.5) demands I be the glue holding this conversation together. If I pause now, the silence feels like rejection or a fight, which my avoidant nature can't handle. Keeping them laughing is the only way I feel safe right now.

goals.description: Scrol through social media for 15 minutes to find a meme that perfectly matches the current joke, ensuring I stay 'in' on the inside humor.
goals.rationale: I'm impulsive and need a quick dopamine hit to avoid the dread of facing my term project alone. Sharing a meme proves I'm still part of the tribe without requiring actual effort. It's safer than opening my laptop and feeling the pressure.

goals.description: Ignore the 'quick_chore' notification completely until the chat thread naturally dies down or someone else leaves.
goals.rationale: Doing this chore now would break the flow and feel like a betrayal of the group vibe. My conflict-avoidant side refuses to be the one who says 'I gotta go' when everyone is having fun. The deadline is three days away; it doesn't matter right this second compared to how much I need this connection.

agent.last_reasoning: (a) Advances 'g1' by keeping group momentum high, satisfying my 0.5 affiliation weight. (b) Avoids the stress of 'quick_chore' or the term project right now; I'm too impulsive to risk breaking this social flow with a task.

goals.description: Immediately text or call cohort_3 to hang out, grab a meal, or just chat to diffuse stress and feel connected.
goals.rationale: My value for relationships (0.5) outweighs everything else. I am avoidant of conflict and isolation; reconnecting now prevents me from feeling 'unreliable' and soothes my immediate anxiety without requiring any hard work.

goals.description: Do the quick_chore (trivial task) right after connecting with friends to get a small dopamine hit and a sense of 'winning' without tackling the big project.
goals.rationale: I have an impulsive need for instant gratification and short-term wins. Doing this trivial task feels productive enough to keep me happy for now, while letting me ignore the term project deadline that is still days away.

goals.description: Ignore the Term Project until Thursday night.
goals.rationale: The future consequences don't matter as much as my current emotional equilibrium. Procrastination is high for me, and I will deal with the stress later rather than risk ruining the moment with friends.

desires: Immediately reconnect with cohort_3 to diffuse my stress and avoid the feeling of being isolated or 'unreliable' by missing out on our bond.
Postpone the term project deadline anxiety for at least a few hours to maintain short-term emotional equilibrium, even if it risks future consequences.

Avoid any potential friction or awkwardness that might arise if I admit I'm falling behind on my work
